emails.subject: Re: ANP Marketing
emails.body: Tana I talked to Jeff Mansfield this a.m. and he implied that they should be receiving a blessing for transacting power. I'm not sure where he got that info, but it must not be true, huh? Thanks, Griff Forwarded by Mary Griff Gray/HOU/ECT on 11/08/2000 08:23 AM Bradley Diebner 11/08/2000 08:01 AM

emails.subject: Re: ANP Marketing
emails.body: I heard back from the power lawyer and they do not appear to have a FERC license. He is going to have to talk to her and clear this up. Mary Griff Gray 11/08/2000 08:29 AM

emails.subject: Re: ANP Marketing

emails.subject: ANP Marketing
emails.body: Tana Thanks for the prompt response. Does this mean that eventually they'll be approved for power, but not at this time? Or, we'll hold up the transaction ID until the power side is approved. Griff From: Tana Jones on 11/08/2000 09:12 AM

emails.subject: ANP Marketing
emails.body: Tana Thanks for the prompt response. Does this mean that eventually they'll be approved for power, but not at this time? Or, we'll hold up the transaction ID until the power side is approved. Griff From: Tana Jones on 11/08/2000 09:12 AM

emails.subject: Re: ANP Marketing
emails.body: I'm talking to their lawyer right now. He's checking with his power team to find out what name their FERC license is in. However, we still do not have a Credit sign off on power yet, so I am also working with ("nagging") the power Credit person to get me some headroom. Let's just say ANP is "evolving" as we speak! Mary Griff Gray 11/08/2000 09:17 AM
